g.data-narration: Method overloading allows multiple methods with the same name but different parameters in the same class.
Method overloading improves code readability and enables compile-time polymorphism for flexible method naming.
Methods must differ in number, type, or order of parameters. Return type alone is NOT sufficient for overloading.
A method signature is the method name plus parameter list. The compiler uses this to differentiate overloaded methods.
g.text: Return type is NOT part of the signature!

g.data-narration: First way to overload: different number of parameters. The add method works with 2 or 3 integers.
g.text: int add(int a, int b) { return a+b; }
int add(int a, int b, int c) { return a+b+c; }
Same method name, different parameter count!

g.data-narration: Second way to overload: different parameter types. The print method handles integers and strings differently.
g.text: Same method name, different data types!

g.data-narration: Third way to overload: different order of parameter types. The method accepts int-string or string-int combinations.
g.text: Same method name, different parameter order!

g.data-narration: Method overloading is resolved at compile time, not runtime. The compiler decides which method to call based on arguments.
g.text: The compiler determines which method to call based on
the arguments passed at compile time.

g.data-narration: Return type alone cannot be used for overloading. These two methods cause a compile-time error.
g.text: int add(int a, int b) { return a+b; }
double add(int a, int b) { return (double)a+b; }

g.data-narration: A real-world example: a Calculator class with overloaded methods to add integers, doubles, and three numbers.
g.text: Same operation, multiple data types supported!

g.data-narration: Key takeaways: overloading enables compile-time polymorphism, improves code readability, and requires different parameters.
Overloading vs overriding: overloading is same class, compile-time, different parameters. Overriding is inheritance, runtime, same signature.
g.text: Don't confuse these two important OOP concepts!

g.data-narration: Common mistakes: thinking return type matters, using same parameter types, and confusing overloading with overriding.
In summary, method overloading provides compile-time polymorphism through multiple methods with same name but different parameters.
g.text: ✓ Same method name in same class
